title: Cooler Weather Hair Extension Care
author: Studio One Hair Design
body_text:
As cooler months arrive in Fresno and Clovis, California, hair faces new seasonal challenges. November through February brings crisp air with highs around 57°F and lows near 40°F, leaving both natural hair and extensions dry, brittle, and dull. Beaded-row and No Sew Luxe extensions require extra care during winter.

When temperatures drop, moisture in the air decreases, stripping natural oils from hair and extensions. Switch to hydrating, sulfate-free shampoos and conditioners like Oribe and Kerasilk that deliver nourishment without weighing extensions down.

Add leave-in conditioner weekly to lock in hydration. Use deep conditioning masks every one to two weeks. Avoid frequent washing—aim for one to two times weekly, using dry shampoo between washes. Before bed, apply lightweight oil to mid-lengths and ends for overnight smoothness.

Cold weather increases breakage, so prevention is essential. Schedule trims every four to eight weeks to remove split ends. Always apply heat protectant spray before styling. When wet, pat hair dry gently with microfiber towels and detangle with wide-tooth combs from ends upward. Maintain consistent extension move-up appointments to keep rows secure and natural hair healthy.

Environmental factors including wind, rain, and indoor heating affect extension quality. Cover hair with cozy hats or silk-lined beanies when outdoors to shield from cold wind and humidity while minimizing friction. If using heaters or fireplaces frequently, run a humidifier to maintain moisture balance. After workouts, wipe away sweat with damp towels before blow-drying roots.

Your hair deserves seasonal care like your skin—hydration, protection, and regular maintenance keep extensions lush all season. Studio One Hair Design offers appointments for move-ups and moisture treatments to help navigate winter with confidence and shine.
